articles.headline: PENNSYLVANIA CONVENTION (Reform Convention: suffrage debate)
articles.author: (abridged from Poulson's American Daily Advertiser)
articles.summary: Day-by-day report of the Pennsylvania Reform (Constitutional) Convention debating the suffrage article. Mr. Martin moved to insert the word 'white' before 'freemen' to confine the franchise to white freemen. Judge Hopkinson argued colored people were not prohibited from voting under the existing constitution but favored the change; Mr. Dunlop of Franklin defended universal emancipation and cited Black Pennsylvanians' century of residence; Mr. Darlington read Albert Gallatin's letter noting 'white' was deliberately omitted from the 1790 constitution, and Gen. Jackson's proclamation calling free men of color 'citizens.' On Saturday Jan. 20, 1838, about 7 p.m., the question was taken: ayes 77, noes 45 — the word 'white' was inserted, disenfranchising Black Pennsylvanians. Scott's amendment to let the legislature re-enfranchise after 1850 was lost (73-36), as was Dunlop's property-holder amendment and Merrill's literacy amendment.

articles.headline: THE ANNIVERSARY (Pennsylvania State Anti-Slavery Society annual meeting)
articles.summary: Brief notice of the Pennsylvania State Anti-Slavery Society annual meeting at Wilson's(?) Hotel saloon, Harrisburg — the same place as last year's convention — with nearly one hundred delegates. President Dr. F. J. LeMoyne took the chair. Resolutions admitted friendly non-delegates as honorary members; committees were appointed on business, evening lectures, and a remonstrance against the annexation of Texas; a letter from Wm. Lloyd Garrison was read; reports of the Western and Eastern District Executive Committees and of the Philadelphia City and County A.S. Society were presented.

articles.headline: CONGRESSIONAL — Calhoun's Resolutions
articles.summary: Full text of Calhoun's resolutions as passed by the U.S. Senate, asserting state sovereignty over 'domestic institutions,' denouncing abolitionist 'intermeddling,' and declaring that abolition in the District of Columbia or the territories would violate the constitutional compact.

articles.headline: [Female agency in moral reformation] (editorial)
articles.summary: Editorial defending women's right to petition and organize against slavery, ridiculing the shifting objections that women are 'out of their sphere' when pleading for two and a half million enslaved Americans though 'in their sphere' when aiding Greeks, Poles, Cherokees, temperance, or foreign missions. Invokes the murdered minister (Lovejoy): 'the blood of a minister of Christ is smoking on the demon's altar... we dare not be dumb.'

articles.headline: GOURITIZA'S PAMPHLET (Congress)
articles.summary: In the House Jan. 18, Mr. Cushing, authorized by British Minister Mr. Fox, disclaimed any British connection with the Gorostiza pamphlet affair concerning Mexico.

events.name: Pennsylvania Reform Convention: 'white freemen' suffrage vote
events.description: Constitutional convention vote (77-45) inserting 'white' into the suffrage article, stripping the vote from Black Pennsylvanians. Debated Jan. 17-22, 1838.

events.name: Pennsylvania State Anti-Slavery Society annual meeting (Anniversary)
events.description: Annual meeting, ~100 delegates; Garrison letter read; Texas annexation remonstrance committee formed.
